reasoning: The Lean formalization captures every element of the informal statement: completeness of the metric space (CompleteSpace X), the sequence of sets (E : ℕ → Set X), closedness (IsClosed), nonemptiness (.Nonempty), boundedness (Bornology.IsBounded — the correct Mathlib predicate), the decreasing chain condition (E n ⊇ E (n+1)), and the diameter limit (Tendsto (Metric.diam ∘ E) atTop (𝓝 0)). The conclusion "∃ a, Set.iInter E = {a}" precisely encodes "the intersection consists of exactly one point" since equality to a singleton {a} means exactly one element. The indexing from 0 vs 1 is immaterial because E 0 ⊇ E 1 makes both intersections equal.
